lesson.menu_desc: Get the Information You Need with Question Words & Indirect Questions -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 9 -- Asking Smart Questions -- Question Words & Indirect Questions
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Simone Quiles de Santana Marques | Aula 9 -- Asking Smart Questions

listenings.text: Hi Simone, this is Daniel. I am calling about the due diligence session on Thursday. The other side will be there to answer our questions about the contract, so this is your chance to ask everything we need. Before the call, I want you to prepare a list of smart questions. Please confirm the key terms, and do not be afraid to ask them to specify the numbers. I was wondering if you could also probe the indemnity clause, because we still have one concern there. If anything is not clear, just ask them to rephrase it. Remember to keep every question relevant to the deal. Could you send me your question list before the meeting? Thank you, and talk soon.

Hello, this is Simone. I am recording my questions for the due diligence session, so I do not forget anything important. First, I would like to confirm the main terms, so I will ask: could you tell me when the contract takes effect? Then I want to specify the numbers, so I will ask: do you know how much the final price is? I am wondering why they changed the indemnity clause, so I will ask them to explain the reason. I also have one concern about the timeline, so I want to ask whether the closing date is final. If an answer is not clear, I will politely ask them to rephrase it. Finally, I will check a few details and make sure every question stays relevant. I think I am ready for Thursday.

extra_audio.text: Daniel leaves a voice note inviting Simone to the due diligence session. Simone prepares a list of smart questions on her prep sheet. They meet on the call and Simone asks her indirect questions. Daniel and the other side answer and confirm the key terms. Simone checks the last details and closes the question session.
